item.title: If you like naan you might enjoy this
item.description:
Mentioned in the recipe: only attempt with stainless steel or cast iron pan! Do not attempt this with a non-stick pan of any kind.

item.description: Welp, this was my first post after 12 years of lurking, and it hit front page. Thanks all. (Do we still do front page edits?) Edit #2 - mods removed original #1. Was too sexy I guess. The comments won't make much sense now.

item.title: It's time for yet another unhinged XL Thursday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings online friends
as is tradition
haters, if you're reading this, the meme is very accurate
one of the best holes out there
the red one is Circuit City
gary oldman everyone gif
always
that's rough (ruff)
make me
i don't like this
i have no qualms with ohio
alright i guess
Rogen
Only who can prevent forest fires? You have selected “you”, referring to me. That is incorrect. The correct answer is you.
she's sensitive ok
mmm hmm
i knew this wasn't right
that's my time.. have a great night gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: A kitty saving another kitty in trouble
item.dc:creator: Inconlystisent

item.title: I make indie games and this happened to me today
item.description: For context: I make indie games and have released two so far and I’m currently working on the third one which is weird as fuck. So the way that Steam works is, they don’t send you money anytime you make a sale, but they send all of it at the end of every month. Now September is almost over and I got an e-mail titled “Steam Payment Notification” and I get all hyped up. I open it and read it that the Payment Notification is actually that there is no Payment since I didn’t make $100 in sales. Way to hype me up and bring me down, Steam.
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

item.title: Eddie Izzard: Blisters and Missing Toenails
item.description: Eddie Izzard photo: By Giuseppe Sollazzo - https://www.flickr.com/photos/puntofisso/17123311876/, CC BY-SA 2.0, https://commons.wikimedia.org/w/index.php?curid=40679228 https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/2.0/ Sauce: Liz Thomas and Jo Clements, “Eddie Izzard triumphantly finishes 1,100 mile marathon around Britain in Trafalgar Square.” Daily Mail. 16 September 2009 http://www.dailymail.co.uk/tvshowbiz/article-1213682/Eddie-Izzard-completes-mega-seven-week-marathon-blisters-blisters.html “Eddie Izzard (born Edward John Izzard; 7 February 1962), also known as Suzy Izzard, is a British stand-up comedian, actor and activist. Her comedic style takes the form of what appears to the audience as rambling whimsical monologues and self-referential pantomime. […] “On 27 July 2009, with only five weeks’ training and no significant prior running experience, Izzard began seven weeks of back-to-back marathon runs (with Sundays off) across the UK to raise money for Sport Relief. She ran from London to Cardiff to Belfast to Edinburgh and back to London, carrying the flag of the country—England, Scotland, or Wales—in which she was running. In Northern Ireland, she carried a self-designed green flag bearing a white dove. The blog Eddie Iz Running documented the 43 marathons in 51 days, covering at least 27 miles per day (totalling more than 1,100 miles), ending on 15 September 2009. Izzard received a special award at BBC Sports Personality of the Year in 2009 for these achievements. In March 2010, she took part in the Sport Relief Mile event.” https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eddie_Izzard
item.dc:creator: oukid

item.title: The world feels a little darker now this light has gone out.

item.dc:creator: SocialKeked

item.title: "Predator Trophy" 3D printed skull and spine.
item.description: I finished this project a couple of months ago and just realized I never shared it on here. This was all 3D printed from files I found on Thingiverse. I had to modify the spine a lot to attach it to the skull and make it flexible, but sturdy. I also put acrylic teeth into the skull to make it more realistic. All of the gore is cloth and permablood. Skull model made by Buzzsawstudio and spine was model from DaveMakesStuff
item.dc:creator: Oaklanecemetery

item.title: Images of Note: letma
item.description:
Sadly true
Futurama premiered on March 28, 1999, so there's a chance, a chance that this could be a joke. Also I wonder if Fry learnt about 9/11?
yes

item.title: His own platform calling out his bullshit.
item.description: Fact checking Elon needs to happen more. He has become unhinged with ego and delusions of influence. Hard to believe the world's richest man chooses to spend his time acting as an internet troll, but heck, that's 2024 baby.
